name: Julióbriga Roman City – circular from Bolmir via La Llanuca
name_original: Julióbriga Ciudad Romana – circular desde Bolmir por La Llanuca
region_intro: Urban hiking trails in Embalse Del Ebro offer routes that combine town exploration with access to natural features. The region is characterized by the vast Embalse del Ebro reservoir and the Ebro River, which flows through the area. Reinosa, a central town, provides starting points for paths that navigate its historic center and adjacent green spaces. These routes typically feature gentle elevation changes, making them accessible for various fitness levels. Best urban hiking trails in Embalse Del Ebro The most popular urban hiking route is Plaza de España, Reinosa – Historic Center of Reinosa loop from Río Ebro, a 4.3 miles (6.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 44 minutes to complete. This route explores the historic core of Reinosa alongside the Ebro River. Another top favourite among local hikers is The Okuda Bear loop from Río Ebro, an easy 4.6 miles (7.4 km) path. This trail features urban art installations and follows the riverbanks. Local hikers also love the Plaza de España, Reinosa – Historic Center of Reinosa loop from Reinosa, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail leading through the historic center of Reinosa, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Urban hiking in Embalse Del Ebro is defined by routes through historic towns, along riverbanks, and near the reservoir, with options suitable for easy walks. The network offers choices for different ability levels, focusing on accessible paths. The routes in Embalse Del Ebro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 250 hikers have used komoot to explore Embalse Del Ebro's varied urban terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many urban hiking trails are available in Embalse Del Ebro?
A: There are currently &lt;strong&gt;3 easy urban hiking trails&lt;/strong&gt; listed in Embalse Del Ebro, perfect for exploring the towns and their surroundings.

Q: What kind of urban hiking experiences can I expect in Embalse Del Ebro?
A: Urban hikes in Embalse Del Ebro offer a blend of cultural exploration and natural beauty. You can expect to walk through historic town centers, along riverbanks, and discover local landmarks. The trails are generally easy, making them accessible for most fitness levels.

Q: Are there any circular urban walking routes in Embalse Del Ebro?
A: Yes, all the urban hiking routes in Embalse Del Ebro are circular. For example, you can enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38179109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de España, Reinosa – Historic Center of Reinosa loop from Río Ebro&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40312918" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Okuda Bear loop from Río Ebro&lt;/a&gt;, both offering convenient starting and ending points.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along urban trails?
A: Urban trails in Embalse Del Ebro often lead you past significant local attractions. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5378470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tower of the Church of San Roque&lt;/a&gt;, also known as 'The Cathedral of the Fish', or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5084696" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain and Old Bridge at Embalse del Ebro&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6907520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ebro Dam&lt;/a&gt; is another notable man-made structure in the vicinity.

Q: Are the urban hiking trails in Embalse Del Ebro suitable for families?
A: Yes, the urban hiking trails in Embalse Del Ebro are generally easy and suitable for families. With distances ranging from approximately 5 to 7 kilometers and minimal elevation gain, they offer a pleasant way to explore the towns and their surroundings together.

Q: What is the best time of year for urban hiking in Embalse Del Ebro?
A: The Embalse del Ebro region offers pleasant conditions for urban hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially in the mornings or late afternoons, while winter walks can be refreshing, though it's advisable to check local weather conditions.

Q: Are there any parks or green spaces along the urban routes?
A: Yes, some urban routes may pass through or near local green spaces. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4259563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Rozas Park&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a facility that could be encountered, offering a pleasant spot for a break or to enjoy nature within an urban setting.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about urban hiking in Embalse Del Ebro?
A: The komoot community rates urban hiking in Embalse Del Ebro highly, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.3 out of 5 stars&lt;/strong&gt;. Reviewers often praise the accessibility of the easy trails, the blend of historical sights with natural river views, and the opportunity to explore local towns like Reinosa.

Q: Are there any specific urban trails that offer views of the Ebro River?
A: Many urban trails in the region are situated close to the Ebro River, offering scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38179109" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de España, Reinosa – Historic Center of Reinosa loop from Río Ebro&lt;/a&gt;, for example, starts and ends near the river, providing opportunities to enjoy its presence.

Q: Where can I find parking for urban hikes in Embalse Del Ebro?
A: For urban hikes, parking is typically available within the towns themselves, such as Reinosa. Look for designated public parking areas near the starting points of the trails. As these are urban routes, accessibility by car is generally good.

Q: Can I reach urban hiking trails in Embalse Del Ebro using public transport?
A: Towns like Reinosa, which serve as starting points for urban hikes, are generally well-connected by public transport within the region. Checking local bus or train schedules for routes to Reinosa or other trail-adjacent towns is recommended for planning your journey.
tips: A few hundred from the Domus is the other part of the Roman city of Julióbriga.
Between the Domus and the church.

The Domus is a reconstruction of a Roman house, based on the remains of a Julióbriga building called "La casa de los Morillos" in which it serves to see how the wealthy classes of the Empire lived.
The Domus is now a house/museum that houses a permanent exhibition, where the most significant objects recovered during the archaeological excavations of the city are exhibited.
A very pretty church, with stairs to access the belfry.
It is freely visited, next to the church.
